title: React Reconciliation
tldr: Reconciliation is the process by which React updates the DOM by comparing the new VDOM tree with the previous one.
React uses a heuristic O(n) algorithm: different element types produce different trees; stable keys identify elements across renders.
When root element types differ, React tears down the old tree and builds a new one from scratch.
Keys on list children allow React to match, reuse, and reorder elements efficiently.
laymanDefinition: Like comparing new photos with current ones on a bulletin board. If the frame changed (element type), replace entirely. For photos in a row, use name tags (keys) to know which stayed, which are new, which were removed.

deepDive.heading: The Two Assumptions
deepDive.text: 1) Different element types produce different trees. 2) Developers provide stable keys for element identity across renders. These enable O(n) complexity.

deepDive.heading: Recursing on Children
deepDive.text: When diffing same-type elements, React recurses on children positionally. Matching type updates existing DOM node. Different type removes old and inserts new.

deepDive.heading: List Reconciliation with Keys
deepDive.text: Keys allow React to match children across renders. React determines insertions, deletions, reorderings, and updates using key identity.

deepDive.heading: Component Instance Reuse
deepDive.text: Same type and key = reuse component instance. State persists (useState), only props update.

deepDive.heading: Step by Step Process
deepDive.text: 1. Render phase: produce new VDOM. 2. Diff: compare element by element. 3. Effect list: insert/update/remove/reorder. 4. Commit: apply to real DOM.

interviewAnswer: Reconciliation is React's algorithm for efficiently updating the DOM when state changes. It compares new VDOM with previous using heuristic O(n) diffing. Same type + key = component instance preserved. Split into render phase (interruptible diffing) and commit phase (synchronous DOM mutations).

interviewQuestions.question: What is reconciliation?
interviewQuestions.answer: The process of updating the DOM to match the new VDOM tree, computing minimal DOM mutations.

interviewQuestions.question: What happens with different element types at same position?
interviewQuestions.answer: React tears down old component and builds new one from scratch. State lost, new DOM subtree inserted.

interviewQuestions.question: How do keys help list reconciliation?
interviewQuestions.answer: Uniquely identify items across renders. React matches, preserves state, efficiently inserts/removes.

interviewQuestions.question: Why is array index as key problematic?
interviewQuestions.answer: Incorrect matching when items are added/removed/reordered. React matches by position, not identity.

interviewQuestions.question: What happens when type and key match?
interviewQuestions.answer: Component instance reused. DOM node updated with new props. Hook state preserved.

interviewQuestions.question: Render phase vs commit phase?
interviewQuestions.answer: Render: compute diff (can pause). Commit: apply mutations (synchronous).

interviewQuestions.question: How to skip unnecessary reconciliation?
interviewQuestions.answer: React.memo, shouldComponentUpdate, useMemo, useCallback prevent re-rendering when props unchanged.

interviewQuestions.question: Wrong key bug?
interviewQuestions.answer: Non-unique/unstable keys cause element mismatch and state corruption. Keys must be stable and unique among siblings.

codeExamples.title: Correct Key Usage
codeExamples.description: Stable keys (task.id) let React optimally reconcile list updates.

codeExamples.title: React.memo for Skipping
codeExamples.description: React.memo skips re-rendering if props haven't changed (shallow comparison).

mcqQuestions.question: What does React.memo do?
mcqQuestions.explanation: Shallow compares props to skip re-render.
